question: [File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.toMarket] Can an unprivileged market creator craft two distinct Market structs whose abi.encode(market) payloads produce the same keccak256 salt in toId, causing a market ID collision that merges accounting (credit, debt, collateral, lossFactor) of two logically separate markets, enabling bad debt creation in one market to slash lenders of the other? Preconditions: permissionless market creation, two markets with different collateralParams arrays that ABI-encode to the same bytes. Call sequence: touchMarket(marketA) -> touchMarket(marketB) -> assert id(marketA) == id(marketB). Invariant tested: distinct Market structs must produce distinct bytes32 IDs. Scoped impact: bad debt socialization across unrelated markets, undercollateralized borrow in merged market. Proof idea: fuzz Market struct fields (loanToken, maturity, collateralParams length, token addresses, lltv, maxLif, oracle, rcfThreshold, enterGate, liquidatorGate) and assert toId(marketA) != toId(marketB) for all distinct pairs.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.toMarket] Can an attacker pre-deploy a contract at the CREATE2 address computed by IdLib.toId (lower 20 bytes of the market ID) before the legitimate market is created, storing crafted bytecode that abi.decode returns a Market with a lower LLTV, a higher maxLif, or a malicious oracle address, so that toMarket() returns wrong risk parameters used in isHealthy and liquidate, enabling undercollateralized borrowing? Preconditions: attacker can compute the CREATE2 address offline before touchMarket is called; CREATE2 deployer is Midnight itself so attacker cannot use the same deployer, but check if any other path allows pre-deployment. Call sequence: attacker computes expected CREATE2 address -> attacker pre-deploys contract at that address via a different deployer -> legitimate user calls touchMarket -> toMarket() reads attacker-controlled bytecode. Invariant tested: toMarket(id) must always return the exact Market struct used at market creation. Scoped impact: health check uses attacker-chosen LLTV/oracle, enabling undercollateralized borrow. Proof idea: unit test pre-deploying a contract at the expected CREATE2 address and assert toMarket reverts or returns correct data.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.storeInCode / Midnight.touchMarket] Can a hard-fork that changes block.chainid cause IdLib.storeInCode (which uses the live block.chainid as CREATE2 salt) to deploy market params to a different address than IdLib.toId (which uses INITIAL_CHAIN_ID) computes, so that toMarket(id) reads from an empty or wrong address and returns garbage Market params (wrong oracle, LLTV, maturity), enabling health checks to pass with incorrect collateral valuations? Preconditions: protocol deployed on chain with chainId=A; hard fork changes chainId to B; new market created post-fork. Call sequence: deploy Midnight on chainId=A (INITIAL_CHAIN_ID=A) -> hard fork to chainId=B -> touchMarket(market) -> storeInCode uses chainId=B as salt -> toId uses INITIAL_CHAIN_ID=A as salt -> CREATE2 addresses diverge -> toMarket reads wrong address. Invariant tested: toMarket(toId(market)) must always return the original market struct. Scoped impact: isHealthy uses wrong LLTV/oracle from garbage-decoded market, enabling undercollateralized borrow or blocking valid liquidation. Proof idea: differential test with two chainId values, assert toId address matches storeInCode deployed address.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId] Can the truncation of the 32-byte market ID to a 20-byte address in toMarket() (address(uint160(uint256(id)))) cause two distinct market IDs that share the same lower 20 bytes to map to the same storage contract, so that toMarket() returns the same Market struct for two different markets, allowing a borrower to use collateral params from market A while accounting runs under market B's ID? Preconditions: two markets whose toId outputs differ only in the upper 12 bytes. Call sequence: touchMarket(marketA) -> touchMarket(marketB) -> assert toMarket(idA) != toMarket(idB) even if address(uint160(uint256(idA))) == address(uint160(uint256(idB))). Invariant tested: market ID to market struct mapping must be injective. Scoped impact: wrong LLTV/oracle used in isHealthy for one market, enabling undercollateralized position. Proof idea: fuzz market params to find ID pairs with matching lower 20 bytes; assert toMarket returns distinct structs.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.isHealthy] Can a market creator construct a Market struct where the collateralParams array ordering (required to be sorted ascending by token address) produces a different abi.encode output than an equivalent market with the same logical collaterals but different index assignments, causing the bitmap index i in isHealthy to reference collateralParams[i] with a different oracle or LLTV than the one used when the borrower supplied collateral at that index, enabling health check bypass? Preconditions: market with N collaterals; borrower supplies collateral at index j; market ID encodes collateralParams in sorted order. Call sequence: touchMarket(market) -> supplyCollateral(market, j, assets, borrower) -> take(offer) to borrow -> assert isHealthy uses collateralParams[j].oracle and collateralParams[j].lltv matching the index used at supply time. Invariant tested: collateral bitmap index i must always correspond to the same collateralParams[i] entry for the lifetime of the market. Scoped impact: wrong oracle price or LLTV used in health check, enabling undercollateralized borrow. Proof idea: unit test with multi-collateral market, verify bitmap index consistency across supply, health check, and liquidation.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.storeInCode / Midnight.toMarket] Can the abi.decode in toMarket() silently succeed but return a Market struct with a zero or address(0) oracle for one or more collateralParams entries if the stored bytecode is shorter than expected (e.g., due to a truncated CREATE2 deployment), causing IOracle(address(0)).price() to be called in isHealthy, which either reverts (DoS) or returns 0 (maxDebt=0, all positions instantly liquidatable)? Preconditions: storeInCode deployment produces truncated bytecode due to gas limit or memory edge case. Call sequence: touchMarket(market) with large collateralParams array -> storeInCode runs out of gas or truncates -> toMarket(id) decodes partial struct -> isHealthy calls oracle at address(0). Invariant tested: toMarket must always return a fully valid Market struct with non-zero oracle addresses. Scoped impact: all borrowers in market become instantly liquidatable (healthy-account liquidation) or market is DoS'd. Proof idea: fuzz collateralParams array length up to MAX_COLLATERALS=128 and assert storeInCode always deploys complete bytecode.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.touchMarket] Can a market creator pass a Market struct with a maturity value that, after abi.encode and keccak256 in toId, produces a market ID whose lower 20 bytes collide with an existing ERC20 token or protocol contract address, causing toMarket() to abi.decode that contract's bytecode as a Market struct and return a garbage maturity, enabling post-maturity liquidation mode to be triggered prematurely or suppressed? Preconditions: attacker can brute-force market params to target a specific CREATE2 address. Call sequence: attacker computes market params such that address(uint160(uint256(toId(market)))) == target_address -> touchMarket(market) -> toMarket(id) decodes target_address.code as Market -> garbage maturity used in liquidate postMaturityMode check. Invariant tested: market maturity used in liquidation must equal the maturity in the original Market struct. Scoped impact: post-maturity liquidation triggered on healthy pre-maturity positions, or post-maturity liquidation blocked on overdue positions. Proof idea: unit test with crafted market params targeting a known address and assert toMarket returns correct maturity.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.isHealthy] Can the abi.encode of a Market struct with a dynamic CollateralParams[] array produce length-extension or padding ambiguities where two Market structs with different collateralParams lengths but same token/lltv/oracle values encode to the same bytes, causing toId to return the same market ID for markets with different collateral counts, so that isHealthy iterates over a different number of collaterals than were validated at market creation? Preconditions: Solidity ABI encoding of dynamic arrays includes length prefix; check if any padding or tail encoding trick can produce collision. Call sequence: touchMarket(marketWith2Collaterals) -> touchMarket(marketWith3Collaterals) -> assert IDs differ. Invariant tested: markets with different collateral counts must have distinct IDs. Scoped impact: health check skips or double-counts collateral, enabling undercollateralized borrow. Proof idea: differential fuzz test encoding of Market structs with varying collateralParams lengths.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.liquidate] Can a market creator set rcfThreshold in the Market struct to a value that, when recovered via toMarket() in liquidate, differs from the value used at market creation (e.g., due to abi.decode rounding or truncation of uint256 fields), causing the RCF dust check to use a wrong threshold, allowing full liquidation of a position that should be partially liquidated (or vice versa), enabling bad debt creation? Preconditions: rcfThreshold is a uint256 field in Market; abi.encode/decode round-trip must be lossless. Call sequence: touchMarket(market with rcfThreshold=X) -> borrow to near-health threshold -> liquidate with seizedAssets that would violate RCF -> assert rcfThreshold used in liquidate equals X. Invariant tested: rcfThreshold recovered from toMarket must equal the value at market creation. Scoped impact: RCF bypass enables over-liquidation, leaving borrower with bad debt. Proof idea: fuzz rcfThreshold values and assert abi.encode/decode round-trip is lossless for all uint256 values.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.storeInCode / Midnight.toMarket] Can the SSTORE2_PREFIX bytecode prefix (hex '600b380380600b5f395ff3') interact with the abi.encode(market) payload in a way that causes the deployed contract's runtime bytecode to be shorter or longer than expected (e.g., if the payload contains bytes that are interpreted as STOP opcodes by the EVM during deployment), causing toMarket() to abi.decode a truncated or extended Market struct with wrong LLTV, oracle, or maturity values? Preconditions: the SSTORE2_PREFIX uses CODECOPY to deploy the payload as runtime code; the payload is raw abi.encode output which may contain 0x00 bytes. Call sequence: touchMarket(market with collateralParams containing specific byte patterns) -> storeInCode deploys -> toMarket reads deployed code -> abi.decode returns wrong struct. Invariant tested: runtime bytecode of CREATE2 contract must exactly equal abi.encode(market). Scoped impact: wrong oracle/LLTV/maturity in recovered market enables undercollateralized borrow or health check bypass. Proof idea: unit test with market params that produce abi.encode payloads containing 0x00 bytes and assert toMarket returns correct struct.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.isHealthy] Can a market creator construct a Market with a collateralParams array where one entry has lltv=WAD (1e18, the special LLTV=1 case) and another has lltv<WAD, and the bitmap index assignment in isHealthy causes the WAD-LLTV collateral's oracle to be used for a different collateral's health calculation, enabling a borrower to borrow against the WAD-LLTV collateral's favorable terms while actually posting the lower-LLTV collateral? Preconditions: multi-collateral market with mixed LLTV tiers; bitmap index i maps to collateralParams[i]. Call sequence: touchMarket(market with collateralParams[0].lltv=0.77e18, collateralParams[1].lltv=1e18) -> borrower supplies collateral at index 0 -> bitmap bit 0 set -> isHealthy uses collateralParams[0].lltv=0.77e18 correctly -> verify no index confusion. Invariant tested: bitmap bit i must always correspond to collateralParams[i] with correct lltv and oracle. Scoped impact: undercollateralized position if index confusion allows WAD-LLTV terms for lower-LLTV collateral. Proof idea: invariant fuzz test asserting collateralBitmap bit i set implies position.collateral[i] > 0 and isHealthy uses collateralParams[i].lltv.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.touchMarket] Can a market creator pass a Market struct where enterGate or liquidatorGate is set to address(0) in the struct but the abi.encode packs it differently than expected (e.g., due to Solidity struct padding), causing the recovered market from toMarket() to have a non-zero gate address that blocks liquidation or entry, effectively freezing funds or preventing valid liquidations? Preconditions: address fields in Market struct are 20 bytes but abi.encode pads to 32 bytes; check if any encoding path could corrupt gate addresses. Call sequence: touchMarket(market with enterGate=address(0)) -> toMarket(id) -> assert recovered market.enterGate == address(0). Invariant tested: gate addresses recovered from toMarket must equal those used at market creation. Scoped impact: liquidation blocked (fund freeze) or entry gate incorrectly applied. Proof idea: unit test round-trip of Market struct with zero and non-zero gate addresses through abi.encode/decode.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.isHealthy] Can the mulDivDown rounding in isHealthy (collateral * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * lltv / WAD) combined with a market ID that maps to a recovered Market with a slightly different LLTV (due to toMarket decode error) cause a position that is exactly at the health boundary to be classified as healthy when it is actually undercollateralized, enabling a borrow that creates bad debt? Preconditions: position at exact health boundary (debt == maxDebt); LLTV recovered from toMarket equals original LLTV. Call sequence: touchMarket(market) -> supplyCollateral -> take to borrow exactly at health limit -> assert isHealthy returns true -> assert no rounding in LLTV recovery causes off-by-one. Invariant tested: isHealthy(market, id, borrower) must use the exact LLTV from the original Market struct without rounding loss. Scoped impact: undercollateralized position created at health boundary. Proof idea: fuzz LLTV values at all allowed tiers and assert abi.encode/decode preserves exact uint256 value.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.storeInCode] Can the CREATE2 deployment in storeInCode fail silently (create2Address == address(0)) in a way that bypasses the require check due to a Solidity assembly bug or gas stipend issue, causing touchMarket to succeed with a market ID that points to address(0), so that toMarket() reads address(0).code (empty bytes) and abi.decode returns a zero-initialized Market struct with zero maturity, zero LLTV, and zero oracle, enabling immediate post-maturity liquidation of all borrowers? Preconditions: storeInCode assembly block uses create2 opcode; require(create2Address != address(0)) should catch failure. Call sequence: touchMarket(market) with gas limit that causes CREATE2 to fail -> assert require reverts -> verify no path allows market creation with address(0) storage contract. Invariant tested: market creation must always succeed in storing market params or revert entirely. Scoped impact: zero-maturity market enables immediate post-maturity liquidation of all positions. [File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.liquidate] Can a market creator set maxLif in a CollateralParams entry to a value that passes the maxLif(lltv, cursor) validation in touchMarket but, when recovered via toMarket() in liquidate, is used in the badDebt calculation (collateral * price / ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE * WAD / maxLif) with a different value due to abi.decode precision loss, causing bad debt to be under-realized (leaving phantom debt) or over-realized (slashing lenders more than actual bad debt)? Preconditions: maxLif is computed as WAD * WAD / (WAD - cursor * (WAD - lltv)); check if any uint256 value passes validation but loses precision in round-trip. Call sequence: touchMarket(market) -> borrow -> price drops -> liquidate(seizedAssets=0, repaidUnits=0) to realize bad debt -> assert badDebt computed with correct maxLif. [File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.isHealthy] Can a borrower with a multi-collateral position (up to 16 active collaterals, bitmap up to 128 bits) trigger a health check where UtilsLib.msb iterates the bitmap in a different order than the collateralParams array index, causing isHealthy to use collateralParams[msb(bitmap)] with a mismatched oracle or LLTV for the actual collateral at that index, enabling an undercollateralized position to appear healthy? Invariant tested: for each bit i set in collateralBitmap, isHealthy must use collateralParams[i].oracle and collateralParams[i].lltv. [File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.touchMarket] Can a market creator pass a Market struct where the collateralParams array has exactly MAX_COLLATERALS=128 entries, causing abi.encode(market) to produce a very large payload that, when stored via storeInCode, exceeds the EVM contract size limit (24576 bytes), causing CREATE2 to return address(0) and the market creation to revert, effectively DoS-ing market creation for large collateral sets? Preconditions: each CollateralParams entry is 4 fields * 32 bytes = 128 bytes; 128 entries = 16384 bytes plus array overhead; total abi.encode size may exceed 24576 bytes. Call sequence: touchMarket(market with 128 collateralParams) -> storeInCode -> CREATE2 fails due to size limit -> require(create2Address != address(0)) reverts. Invariant tested: touchMarket must succeed for any valid Market struct with up to MAX_COLLATERALS collaterals. Scoped impact: DoS of market creation for maximum collateral count, preventing valid market setup. Proof idea: unit test touchMarket with 128 collateralParams and measure deployed bytecode size.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.isHealthy] Can a market creator set the oracle address in a CollateralParams entry to a contract that returns different prices depending on whether it is called from isHealthy (view) or liquidate (non-view), and verify that the market ID derived from toId correctly binds this oracle address so that the health check and liquidation use the same oracle, preventing a scenario where a position appears healthy to isHealthy but is liquidatable in liquidate? Preconditions: oracle address is part of the Market struct encoded in toId; oracle behavior is attacker-controlled. Call sequence: touchMarket(market with manipulable oracle) -> borrow -> oracle returns high price in isHealthy -> oracle returns low price in liquidate -> assert liquidation correctly uses same oracle as health check. Invariant tested: oracle address used in isHealthy and liquidate must be the same as encoded in the Market struct at creation. Scoped impact: healthy-account liquidation if oracle returns different values per call context. Proof idea: unit test with a mock oracle that returns different values in view vs non-view context.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.touchMarket] Can a market creator pass a Market struct with maturity=block.timestamp (exactly at creation time), causing the market to be immediately post-maturity, so that any borrow attempt reverts with CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity but all existing positions (if any could be created) are immediately liquidatable in post-maturity mode with LIF starting at 1, enabling zero-incentive liquidations that realize bad debt without adequate collateral seizure? Preconditions: maturity <= block.timestamp at touchMarket time; maturity <= block.timestamp + 100*365 days check passes for maturity=block.timestamp. Call sequence: touchMarket(market with maturity=block.timestamp) -> attempt take to borrow -> assert CannotIncreaseDebtPostMaturity -> assert any pre-existing debt is liquidatable with lif=1. Invariant tested: markets with maturity=block.timestamp must not allow debt creation and must correctly apply post-maturity LIF=1. Scoped impact: bad debt creation if LIF=1 liquidation cannot cover debt. Proof idea: unit test with maturity=block.timestamp and assert correct LIF computation in liquidate.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.isHealthy] Can a borrower exploit the fact that isHealthy does not call any oracle when debt==0 (early return with maxDebt=0 >= debt=0) to supply collateral, take a borrow, repay to debt=0, then withdraw all collateral, and then take another borrow in the same transaction via multicall, bypassing the health check because the intermediate debt=0 state skips oracle calls? Proof idea: stateful fuzz test with multicall sequences and assert debt > 0 implies isHealthy == true at end of transaction.

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.liquidate] Can a liquidator pass a collateralIndex that is set in the borrower's collateralBitmap but whose corresponding collateralParams[collateralIndex].oracle returns a price of 0, causing seizedAssets = repaidUnits * lif / WAD * ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE / 0 to revert with division by zero, permanently blocking liquidation of an unhealthy position and allowing bad debt to accumulate without realization? [File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.isHealthy] Can a market creator construct a Market where two collateralParams entries have the same token address but different oracles or LLTVs (bypassing the sorted-ascending check since they would be equal, not strictly greater), causing the bitmap to track two entries for the same token, and isHealthy to double-count that token's collateral value, enabling a borrower to appear overcollateralized with half the actual collateral? [File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.supplyCollateral / Midnight.isHealthy] Can a borrower supply collateral at index i, have the collateralBitmap bit i set, then have the market creator (or anyone) create a new market with the same ID but different collateralParams[i].oracle (impossible if ID is collision-resistant, but test the assumption), causing subsequent isHealthy calls to use a different oracle for the same bitmap bit, enabling the borrower's position to appear healthy with a manipulated price? [File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.liquidate] Can the post-maturity LIF ramp computation (WAD + (maxLif - WAD) * (block.timestamp - market.maturity) / TIME_TO_MAX_LIF) overflow uint256 for extreme maxLif values or large time deltas, causing lif to wrap around to a small value, so that seizedAssets = repaidUnits * lif / WAD * ORACLE_PRICE_SCALE / liquidatedCollatPrice computes a near-zero seizure for a large repayment, enabling a liquidator to repay debt without receiving adequate collateral (bad deal for liquidator) or enabling a borrower to have debt repaid for free? Call sequence: touchMarket(market with maxLif=maxLif(LLTV_0, LIQUIDATION_CURSOR_HIGH)) -> warp past maturity + TIME_TO_MAX_LIF -> liquidate in postMaturityMode -> assert lif == maxLif. Invariant tested: post-maturity LIF must be clamped to [1, maxLif] and never overflow. Scoped impact: overflow in LIF causes incorrect seizure amount, enabling bad debt or free debt repayment. Proof idea: fuzz block.timestamp - market.maturity and maxLif values and assert lif stays in [WAD, maxLif].

[File: 'src/libraries/IdLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Bad debt creation or undercollateralized position creation through incorrect health checks, oracle handling, LLTV/LIF math, maturity logic, or collateral bitmap accounting'] [Function: IdLib.toId / Midnight.isHealthy] Can a borrower with MAX_COLLATERALS_PER_BORROWER=16 active collaterals trigger a health check where the bitmap iteration via UtilsLib.msb processes bits in descending order (highest index first), and if the market's collateralParams array has fewer than 16 entries but the bitmap has bits set beyond the array length, cause an out-of-bounds array access in market.collateralParams[i] that reverts, permanently blocking health checks and liquidations for that borrower? Preconditions: market has N < 16 collateralParams; borrower has bitmap bit set at index >= N; this should be prevented by supplyCollateral using collateralIndex < collateralParams.length. Call sequence: supplyCollateral(market, collateralIndex=N, assets) -> assert revert if collateralIndex >= market.collateralParams.length. Invariant tested: collateralBitmap bits must only be set for valid collateralParams indices. Scoped impact: if reachable, health check revert blocks liquidation of unhealthy position. Proof idea: unit test supplyCollateral with out-of-bounds collateralIndex and assert revert.
